description:
GOAL: Make GC actually run from JIT'd code. Currently `gc()` is invoked only
from Rust tests (search ir_codegen.rs: every gc() call site is `#[test]` or
test-helper). JIT'd code allocates indefinitely without collecting. This
ticket bundles two tightly-coupled pieces — safepoint placement AND the
frame-chain root walker — because they are useless apart.

## Decisions captured (user, 2026-05-11)

- Safepoint strategy: HYBRID (option 1d).
  - Trampoline-tick check is the primary safepoint. Before each frame
    dispatch in `ir_codegen.rs:97-127`, check the current process's heap
    occupancy. If over threshold, run GC before reading the next schema_id
    and calling the next fn.
  - Alloc-edge bumps a soft "over-threshold" flag on the heap. Each
    `fz_alloc_*` runtime fn (struct, list, bitstring, vec*, map, closure,
    float, frame) sets the flag when occupancy crosses the threshold during
    that allocation. The trampoline reads the flag and triggers GC at the
    next tick. No GC during the FFI fn itself — keeps allocations
    re-entrance-safe and avoids GC running mid-builder-state (see MAP_BUILDER
    discussion under fz-ul4.11.32).
- Root-walking strategy: SCHEMA SLOT-KIND (option 2a).
  - Each Schema's FieldDescriptor.kind is extended (or a parallel "gc kind"
    table is built) so every slot is tagged exactly one of:
      - FzValue       — traced as a tagged 64-bit value (current GC walker
                        already knows how to follow these by tag)
      - RawBytes(n)   — opaque, never a root
      - ContPtr       — pointer to next frame; structural, walker recurses
                        through it but doesn't treat it as a user-FzValue
                        root
  - The walker starts at the current frame ptr held by the trampoline (post
    fz-ul4.11.32, the ptr lives on Process). It reads each frame's schema,
    iterates fields, emits FzValue slots into the root set, and chains to
    the next frame via the ContPtr slot. Halts when ContPtr is null
    (top-of-stack).

## Why bundled, not split

Safepoints with no root walker means the trampoline calls gc(roots=[]) and
frees everything, breaking correctness. A walker with no safepoint means
nothing calls it. The trampoline-tick path is the only consumer of the
walker; the walker is the only valid root source for the safepoint. One
commit, one design, lands together.

## Deliverables

### Schema slot-kind extension (src/heap.rs)

```rust
pub enum FieldKind {
    FzValue,            // 8 bytes, tagged
    RawBytes(u32),      // n bytes, opaque
    ContPtr,            // 8 bytes, *mut HeapHeader to next frame
}
```

Audit existing schema builders:
- `build_frame_schema` (ir_codegen.rs:1454-1468): currently emits fields
  by entry-param count. Tag each param's kind per the IR Var's runtime
  representation. Cont slot at frame[16] is ContPtr.
- Tuple / struct / closure / list cons / bitstring / map / vec*
  schemas: re-audit FieldKind tags. Most user slots are FzValue. RawBytes
  is used for VecI64 payload, VecF64 payload, VecU8, VecBit, Float box
  payload, Pid u32 fields (post-.19.2), etc.

### Frame-chain root walker (new in src/heap.rs or new src/gc_roots.rs)

```rust
pub fn collect_roots_from_frame_chain(
    cur: *mut HeapHeader,
    schemas: &SchemaRegistry,
) -> Vec<FzValue>;
```

Walks `cur` backward via each frame's ContPtr slot. For each frame, reads
its schema, emits FzValue-typed slots. Returns the union root set. Safe
under cycles (frames form a list, not a graph — no cycles structurally,
but guard with a visited-set anyway for paranoia).

### Trampoline integration (src/ir_codegen.rs:97-127)

```rust
loop {
    if cur.is_null() { break; }
    if process.heap.should_gc() {           // reads over-threshold flag OR
                                            // tests occupancy
        let roots = collect_roots_from_frame_chain(cur, &process.heap.schemas);
        process.heap.gc(&roots);
        process.heap.clear_should_gc_flag();
    }
    // existing dispatch ...
}
```

NOTE: this depends on fz-ul4.11.32 having landed (Process holds the heap).
If .11.32 hasn't landed yet, the integration uses thread-local HEAP via
`HEAP.with(|h| h.borrow_mut().should_gc())`. Either way, the trampoline
performs the check.

### Alloc-edge over-threshold flag (src/heap.rs)

```rust
pub struct Heap {
    // existing fields...
    over_threshold: Cell<bool>,
    gc_threshold_bytes: usize,   // configurable, default e.g. 1MiB
}

impl Heap {
    pub fn alloc_*(...) -> *mut u8 {
        let p = /* existing alloc */;
        if self.live_bytes() >= self.gc_threshold_bytes {
            self.over_threshold.set(true);
        }
        p
    }
    pub fn should_gc(&self) -> bool { self.over_threshold.get() }
    pub fn clear_should_gc_flag(&self) { self.over_threshold.set(false); }
}
```

### Tests

- Allocate beyond threshold in JIT'd code; observe GC fired (instrument via
  a per-Heap "gc_run_count" counter; assert >= 1 after a hot loop that
  allocates).
- Allocations survive across GC: a hot loop that builds a list of N cons
  cells, where the list is held by an entry-block frame slot tagged
  FzValue, completes correctly and the head is reachable post-GC.
- Slot-kind correctness: a frame whose schema mis-tags an int slot as
  FzValue would cause GC to chase a bogus pointer; add a regression test
  that intentionally builds such a schema and asserts the walker rejects
  or correctly handles it (sanity check).
- ContPtr chaining: build a 100-deep call chain; walker emits roots from
  every frame.
- Over-threshold flag clears: after a GC tick, allocations resume without
  spamming GC every frame.

## Risks

- Schema mis-tagging causes either over-marking (extra retention, perf hit)
  or under-marking (use-after-free, correctness bug). Mitigation:
  exhaustive audit of schema builders; one regression test per
  HeapKind / schema source.
- The trampoline-tick approach pauses ALL frames when the current process's
  heap is full. Under fz-ul4.19 this is per-process and bounded; in
  single-process mode pre-.19, it pauses the whole VM. Documented.
- Cell<bool> for the flag is single-threaded; under multi-thread scheduler
  (post-.19.1) it becomes AtomicBool. Note in body for future.

## Out of scope

- Generational nursery (follow-up).
- Concurrent / incremental GC (follow-up).
- Write barriers (no mutation in fz; not needed).
- Multi-thread atom-flag (covered when multi-thread scheduler lands).

## References

- src/heap.rs:73-96 (Heap struct), :414-436 (current gc() entry), :446-515
  (trace walker — extend to handle ContPtr structurally)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:97-127 (trampoline loop)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:391-393 (the original "frames don't yet root-trace"
  comment that deferred this work)
- src/heap.rs:43-71 (SchemaRegistry, FieldDescriptor)
- fz-ul4.11.32 (per-Process FFI ctx — pairs with this ticket)

## Acceptance

- `gc_run_count` increases under hot allocation in JIT.
- No regressions in existing SAMPLE-based tests.
- One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.31.
title: Real GC: trampoline-tick safepoints + frame-chain root walker
